summary: Large mechanical cleanup of Fighter-related struct offset annotations and a small bitfield-access simplification. The main change was in src/melee/ft/types.h, where offsets in ftCommonData and Fighter were reformatted from generic 0x comments to clearer relative forms such as `/* +8 */`, `/* fp+2228 */`, and bit positions like `/* fp+2228:3 */`. The PR also flattened `x2228_flag` access from `x2228_flag.bits.bN` to `x2228_flag.bN` because the wrapper `bits` struct was removed, and reordered several fighter includes, apparently alphabetically. There were no PR body comments, review comments, or explicit rationale beyond the title, so the intent is inferred from the diff.
what_changed: Reformatted many offset comments in `src/melee/ft/types.h`, especially `ftCommonData` and `Fighter`, from forms like `/* 0x2228 */` to relative forms like `/* fp+2228 */` or `/* +2228 */` depending on context.
Added explicit bit-offset comments for bitfields, for example `/* fp+2225:0 */ u8 x2225_b0 : 1;` and `/* fp+2228:3 */ u8 b3 : 2;`.
Changed the `x2228_flag` layout in `Fighter` from a nested `struct { struct { ... } bits; } x2228_flag;` to a direct bitfield struct with members `b0`, `b1`, `b2`, `b3`, `b5`, `b6`, and `b7`.

Updated all uses of `x2228_flag.bits.bN` in touched C files to `x2228_flag.bN`, including fighter initialization, motion-state handling, draw/common logic, cliff checks, Sandbag OnLoad, and ft_0877 helper code.
Kept the note that `UnkFlagStruct does not quite work` for `x2228_flag`, but moved it to a standalone doc comment above the direct bitfield struct.
Reordered numerous local fighter includes in Donkey Kong and Master Hand files, and one include pair in `ftdata.c`, with no apparent semantic code changes.
smart_moves: The cleanup made offset comments encode the base object explicitly: `fp+...` for Fighter fields avoids ambiguity when reading a large embedded layout.
Bitfield comments now document both byte offset and bit position, which is especially useful around dense flag regions such as `x221C` through `x222A`.
Removing the unnecessary `bits` wrapper from `x2228_flag` reduced call-site noise while preserving the important warning that this flag byte cannot simply use `UnkFlagStruct`.
The PR updated call sites consistently across the fighter subsystem, preventing a half-migrated header/API mismatch.
Include reordering was separated from logic changes; the diff shows only line movement for those files, making the cleanup easy to audit.
decomp_lessons: For large decomp structs, prefer offset comments that state the relevant base pointer, such as `fp+594`, instead of bare `0x594` when the struct is usually accessed through a named pointer.
For bitfields, record byte and bit positions directly in comments (`fp+2228:5`) so future renames can preserve layout knowledge.
When a flag byte has a nonstandard bit grouping, such as `x2228_flag.b3` being two bits wide, model it directly instead of forcing it through a generic flag helper type.
Mechanical header cleanups can require broad source updates if field paths change; search all call sites before merging a struct layout simplification.
Alphabetical include ordering in fighter character files appears to be favored by this cleanup and can reduce churn in future PRs.
naming_conventions: Fighter-relative struct comments use the form `/* fp+OFFSET */`.
Non-Fighter common-data offset comments use the form `/* +OFFSET */`.
Bitfield offset comments use the form `/* fp+OFFSET:BIT */` or equivalent byte-plus-bit notation.
Unknown fields retain offset-derived names such as `x2228_flag`, `x2229_b5_no_normal_motion`, and `x8_someStickThreshold` until better semantic names are known.
For direct bitfields inside special flag structs, member names remain compact `b0`, `b1`, `b2`, etc., while the containing field carries the offset-derived identity.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR appears to be layout/comment and accessor cleanup rather than a matching fix; no assembly-specific matching tactic is evidenced.
The `x2228_flag` direct bitfield struct preserves the same bit widths, including a two-bit `b3`, which should avoid changing generated code while simplifying source access.
Call-site replacements from `.bits.bN` to `.bN` are mechanical and should be safe when the surrounding bitfield layout is unchanged.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available; conclusions are based on title and diff only.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further rename `x2228_flag` or identify meanings for bits b0 through b7.
Search for remaining bare `/* 0x... */` comments in Fighter-related structs that were not converted to `fp+...` style.
Check whether other special flag bytes still use unnecessary `.bits` wrappers and can be simplified safely.
Look for project conventions around include ordering to confirm whether alphabetical local includes became the standard after this PR.
